howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: Simple, transparent, efficient
howItWorks.pageSubtitle: A step-by-step guide to shipping smarter
howItWorks.shipperIntro: Send goods anywhere in Europe — even small volumes
howItWorks.carrierIntro: Browse loads, propose to carry, and grow your business

step1.title: Create your account
step1.description: Sign up in minutes with your company details. Quick verification keeps everyone safe.

step2.title: Post your load
step2.description: Tell us where you're shipping from, where to, and how many pallets. We'll handle the matching.

step3.title: Get matched with carriers
step3.description: Receive offers from verified carriers who are already driving your route. Compare prices easily.

step4.title: Track and communicate
step4.description: Follow your shipment in real-time. Message your carrier directly if needed.

step5.title: Confirm delivery and pay
step5.description: When goods arrive safely, confirm delivery. Payment is released to the carrier automatically.

step1.title: Create your account
step1.description: Register your company and complete a quick verification. Build trust with shippers.

step2.title: Browse available loads
step2.description: See all open shipments from verified shippers. Filter by origin, destination, and timing.

step3.title: Propose to carry
step3.description: Found a load that fits? Submit your offer with your price. Shippers choose the best match.

step4.title: Communicate and coordinate
step4.description: Chat with shippers directly. Agree on pickup times and any special requirements.

step5.title: Deliver and get paid
step5.description: Complete the delivery. Once confirmed, payment is sent directly to you — no delays.

optionalRoutes.title: Optionally: Share your route
optionalRoutes.description: Publish your planned trips to receive matching opportunities automatically. Routes are optional — browsing loads is always available.

howItWorks.supportSubtitle: Prefer to talk to someone? Our team is happy to help.
howItWorks.supportText: We understand that new platforms can feel unfamiliar. Our support team speaks your language and is available during business hours.

impactPage.title: Impact & Responsibility
impactPage.subtitle: This document outlines the market inefficiencies that FreightShare addresses, the design decisions embedded in the platform, and their social and environmental implications. Impact is framed as a consequence of better market structure — not as a separate initiative.

problem.title: The Market Problem
problem.fragmentation: European road freight is characterised by significant fragmentation. Over 90% of road haulage firms in the EU operate fewer than 10 vehicles, creating a highly dispersed supply base with limited visibility into available capacity. (Source: European Commission, EU Road Freight Transport Sector, 2022)
problem.emptyMiles: Industry estimates indicate that 20–30% of truck-kilometres in Europe are driven empty or with partial loads. This represents a structural inefficiency in how capacity is matched to demand, resulting in higher costs per shipment and unnecessary fuel consumption. (Source: IRU, European Commission Road Freight Statistics)
problem.smeAccess: Small and medium enterprises typically pay 15–30% more for equivalent logistics services compared to large shippers. This premium reflects reduced bargaining power, limited access to spot markets, and reliance on intermediaries for capacity sourcing. (Source: EU SME Logistics Access Studies, OECD)
problem.opacity: Pricing in road freight remains largely opaque. Freight rates are often negotiated bilaterally, making it difficult for smaller participants to benchmark costs or identify market-rate pricing. Intermediary margins of 10–25% are common in brokered transactions, adding cost without adding transport capacity. (Source: Freight Forwarding Industry Reports, Ti Insights)

design.title: Design Choices
design.intro: The platform's architecture reflects four structural decisions intended to address the inefficiencies described above. These are not features in the marketing sense — they are constraints and mechanisms that shape how transactions occur.
design.choice1Desc: Shippers and carriers transact directly. There is no intermediary layer capturing margin between the parties. This reduces total transaction cost and increases price transparency for both sides.
design.choice2Desc: All pricing is disclosed before commitment. Carriers see shipper budgets or submit offers that shippers can compare. There is no hidden negotiation or relationship-based pricing advantage.
design.choice3Desc: Shipments can be posted at pallet level, not just full truckloads. This allows carriers to consolidate partial loads and makes professional logistics accessible to shippers with smaller volumes.
design.choice4Desc: Carriers can publish planned routes and receive matching load suggestions. This mechanism prioritises filling existing capacity over creating new trips, reducing deadhead kilometres.

social.title: Social Impact
social.access: By enabling direct access to a pool of verified carriers, the platform reduces the structural disadvantage faced by SMEs. Smaller shippers can access the same market as larger players without requiring dedicated logistics staff, broker relationships, or volume commitments.
social.pricing: Price transparency benefits both sides. Shippers see multiple offers and can benchmark rates. Carriers compete on price and reliability rather than relationship access. This creates a more efficient market with lower information asymmetry.
social.intermediaries: Disintermediation means more of the transaction value stays with the shipper and carrier. This is particularly significant for independent carriers, who often depend on brokers for load access and accept lower net rates as a result.

environmental.title: Environmental Impact
environmental.intro: Road freight accounts for approximately 6% of total EU greenhouse gas emissions and around 27% of transport-related CO₂ emissions. (Source: European Environment Agency, 2023). Efficiency gains in this sector have direct environmental implications.
environmental.capacity: Matching loads to existing routes improves truck utilisation. Higher load factors mean fewer vehicle-kilometres are required to move the same volume of goods. This is the primary mechanism through which the platform contributes to emissions reduction.
environmental.emptyTrips: Route-based matching addresses the empty-running problem directly. Carriers returning from deliveries can find backhaul loads, reducing the proportion of kilometres driven without cargo.
environmental.emissions: These efficiency gains translate to lower emissions per tonne-kilometre. Importantly, this is achieved through better use of existing vehicles, not by adding transport capacity. The environmental benefit is a consequence of operational efficiency, not a separate sustainability initiative.

disclaimer.title: What FreightShare Does Not Claim
disclaimer.text1: FreightShare does not claim to solve climate change, decarbonise logistics, or transform the freight industry. The platform operates within existing infrastructure and makes incremental improvements through better matching and price transparency.
disclaimer.text2: Environmental benefits are proportional to platform adoption and the degree to which matches improve on counterfactual outcomes. We do not overstate impact or make claims that cannot be measured.
disclaimer.text3: The figures cited in this document are drawn from public sources and represent industry-wide estimates, not platform-specific measurements. Where data is uncertain, ranges are provided.

cta.text: For details on how the platform works, see

utilisation.description: Target average utilisation for matched shipments on the platform.
utilisation.caveat: Measured as pallet slots filled vs. available capacity per matched route. Baseline industry average is ~60–70%.

emptyReduction.description: Reduction in deadhead kilometres for carriers using route-based matching.
emptyReduction.caveat: Compared to carrier-reported pre-platform behaviour. Subject to route corridor density and adoption.

smeSavings.description: Target savings for SME shippers compared to broker-intermediated rates.
smeSavings.caveat: Dependent on route availability and competitive carrier supply. Measured post-platform vs. pre-platform benchmarks.

emissions.description: Emissions intensity reduction per tonne-kilometre for platform-matched shipments.
emissions.caveat: To be calculated once sufficient transaction volume is reached. Methodology will follow GHG Protocol Scope 3 guidance.

whyFreightShare.title: Logistics Built for SMEs — Not for Intermediaries
whyFreightShare.scaleSubtitle: European road logistics operates far below its potential. These gaps create cost and competitive disadvantages across the supply chain.

whyFreightShare.mechanismSubtitle: A straightforward mechanism that connects available capacity with shipping demand.

step1.title: SMEs ship by pallet
step1.description: Post any shipment size — from a single pallet to partial loads. No full truckload required.

step2.title: Carriers post routes
step2.description: Transport companies publish existing routes with available pallet capacity.

step3.title: Loads match to routes
step3.description: Shipments are matched onto routes already running — no new trucks added.

step4.title: Transparent payment
step4.description: Pricing is visible upfront. Payments are processed securely on the platform.

whyFreightShare.efficiencySubtitle: Environmental benefits are a direct consequence of optimization — not a separate initiative.

betterLoadFactors.title: Better load factors
betterLoadFactors.description: When trucks run closer to capacity, fewer empty kilometers are traveled per unit shipped.

noAdditionalVehicles.title: No additional vehicles
noAdditionalVehicles.description: FreightShare fills capacity on trucks already running. We don't add new vehicles to the road.

lowerEmissions.title: Lower emissions per unit
lowerEmissions.description: Increasing average truck utilization by just 10% reduces CO₂ emissions per unit shipped proportionally.

whyFreightShare.valueSubtitle: FreightShare creates opportunities for SME shippers and carriers equally.
